threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused trying to decipher the bus routes. Locals know it’s all about the shortcuts through the backstreets.
Caught a late 59 last night, bloke in a full suit swaying with a bouquet. Turns out it was for a surprise engagement. Could smell the champagne!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a barista serve a latte to a dog on a leash while the owner texted in perfect German. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that serves vegan versions of everything, even Döner. It’s somehow both hipster and traditional.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they’ve got a favorite taco spot that’s not Lechita or La Taqueria, and they can argue why it’s better.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a lady serenading folks at the Civic Center stop, only to pause mid-song as a dog joined her on the tracks. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all bohemian charm, but they miss how many locals still prefer the quiet life over the party vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they hesitate at traffic lights; locals don’t wait for green, we’ve learned the shortcuts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the stop instead of just walking to the next corner for the 38. Everyone knows it’s quicker that way.
Last night on the 50, a man serenaded us with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall' while the driver pretended not to hear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of grilled kebab wafting through my apartment window while I hear kids playing outside—only then did Neukölln feel like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find Döner shops that double as late-night therapy sessions—where the kindest kebab makers know your life stories.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Real locals know that the only thing worse than a late bus is a group of tourists blocking the sidewalk while taking selfies. It’s an art form, really.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I saw a guy in a tutu and flip-flops direct Muni at Civic Center. Everyone just nodded like it was normal. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plaça, but it’s the narrow side streets filled with art galleries and quirky bars that capture the real vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still scan every menu at a restaurant, waiting for a translation. Locals just order with a nod and a smile.
